text: Our Q3 revenue grew by 15% year-over-year, exceeding market expectations, though supply chain headwinds remain.
Gross margin declined from 42% to 38% due to higher input costs, while full-year revenue still increased by 9%.
The company ended the quarter with 1.2 billion dollars in cash and reduced short-term debt by 18%.
Management lowered next-quarter revenue guidance by 6% because of slower demand in two regional markets.
A new product line reached 250 million dollars in first-quarter sales, but launch costs reduced operating profit.
The company completed a cost savings program expected to reduce annual expenses by 120 million dollars.
Customer churn increased from 4% to 6%, while average revenue per account grew by 11%.
Market share increased from 12% to 14% in the core segment, supported by higher enterprise demand.
The company expects 80 million dollars in new compliance costs, but no major revenue impact is expected.
Net income recovered from a 30 million dollar loss last year to a 45 million dollar profit this quarter.
The company refinanced 600 million dollars of debt at a lower interest rate, reducing expected annual interest expense by 22 million dollars.
Inventory increased by 19% ahead of peak season, while management expects demand to normalize in the next quarter.
The company signed a three-year enterprise contract worth 320 million dollars, with implementation costs expected in the first year.
Research and development spending rose by 24%, while management expects no material revenue contribution until next year.
Free cash flow increased from 90 million dollars to 160 million dollars, mainly due to improved working capital management.
